rag_rules: Fix unresolved identifiers or invalid helpers in AGREE guarantees by ensuring all referenced names are visible or properly declared in the same annex.
When AGREE reports type errors, align helper declarations with the expected expression types (bool, int, real).
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference visible AADL features or locally declared helpers to avoid type resolution errors.
Use only scalar types int, real, and bool in AGREE const and eq declarations to prevent reference resolution failures.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE guarantees must have a boolean left side in implication expressions.
Component type annexes should avoid implementation-specific subcomponent paths in favor of visible feature relations.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE that are not present in the AADL model's configured dependencies.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid variables or outputs.
